title: TP-Link Tapo P110 Mini Energy Monitoring Smart Plug $11 + Delivery ($0 C&C/ in-Store) @ Bunnings Warehouse
body:
Deal: TP-Link Tapo P110 Mini Energy Monitoring Smart Plug $11 + Delivery ($0 C&C/ in-Store) @ Bunnings Warehouse

[drodgers] Note: this is the older version without Matter support. Matter variant is P110M
[Chh] Quick google search returned:
Matter support means the smart plug uses the universal Matter smart home standard, aka works with apple Home, Google Home, Amazon Alexa, or Home Assistant.
Instead of being locked to just the Tapo App.
Don't mind me I thought this was a question….
[Dabbler68] More than that. With Matter devices you can keep it entirely within your home. No need to access any cloud services. The way smart devices all should be.
[FilthyAngels] those that mind don't Matter, those that Matter don't mind
[whyisave] @A female dog: Real Eyes Realize Real Lies
Tell-Lie-Vision
[jaboday423] https://www.bunnings.com.au/tp-link-tapo-p110m-energy-monito…
Available for $24
[Buyingcrap] https://www.ikea.com/au/en/p/grillplats-plug-with-remote-con…
I use these via home assistant with a google streamer that I already had as the hub, cheaper, works with matter and you get a two button remote that can control 6 functions (singe press, double and long on each button).
[noz] Only thing is that these are much wider in size. I have Inspellning - the previous model with true Zigbee support. It's excellent, but yeah pretty big.
[shodan] These are also the ones that have the problem where with enough prolonged power going through them, they'll fail and rapidly flip off and on (had it happen to 2 of them).
The M variants apparently don't have this issue (not sure if the power stays off when they die, or are more tolerable of higher loads)
[paddling-puddling12] Same experience with rapid flipping on and off, happened to 3 of mine, luckily didn’t break the appliances that were plugged into them. Failure is due to a cheap capacitor failing on the circuit board. Have moved over to IKEA Inspelning plugs with no issues since.
[hopper] Noob here. Can i use this to track usage of my 4 door hisense fridge?
[truongster] Yes, just not via Matter. Can track it via their App or Home assistant.
[9839002] I've got an older version of these and real time watts is accurate but wh and kWh over time is buggy as and I don't necessarily trust it.
Can't say about these models but would love it if someone who's had both can comment if this suffers the same issue
[bamzero] I've got an old Kasa HS110 on my fridge. Same thing, wattage might be ok but kWh aint even close. Says my fridge has used 1.62kWh over the last 30 days.. I wish it was that efficient… I don't remember it's consumption data being that bad though..
[furyou] I have one looks exactly the same but from China. Using for exactly my freezer in the garage. Monitor in ha, and smart life app.
For some reason the plug fails. Completely dies.
But taken off the freezer, plugged in elsewhere, comes back to life.
[ginger-gin] I think it's only can monitor 2 door fridge, 4 door you need to buy 2
[debo] The idea of Matter as I understand it, is to simplify all the 'smart things' apps. Early adopters in this space need to use many apps to do the initial set up of the smart thing e.g. I have Kogan Smarterhome app for some things, then Tuya app which looks after other things. If I was to buy these plugs then I would need the TP-link app to configure. Google Home then links to each of these apps so they can all be seen/used in Google Home (or Alexa/Apple). Matter aims to rationalise all these first level apps so if a thing is Matter compliant you know it will be immediately seen in Google Home. Matter seems to be slow in the uptake though.
[dust2k] Matter is an interoperability protocol and communication standard, similar to Zigbee, MQTT, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth. Consequently, a smart device that supports Matter can natively communicate and integrate with any other Matter-compatible product.
[k4vinD] You can get 4 pack from JB for 38$ that will be 9.5$ https://www.jbhifi.com.au/products/tp-link-tapo-mini-smart-p…
[sando] That's a 4-pack of P100 - it has no energy monitoring.
[Sunnyoz] 4 pack at Officeworks for $68 with Matter
https://www.officeworks.com.au/shop/officeworks/p/tp-link-ta…
[Hangryuman] How do these compare to the Arlec Wi-Fi plug-through monitors with app ? e.g. -
https://www.bunnings.com.au/arlec-white-grid-connect-smart-p…
I bought these on special for around $10 each, and have them plugged through for my split air-con, fridge, and TV - I had them on other devices that used so little power I stopped paying attention to their usage.
[dust2k] Wi-Fi-based smart devices typically have higher power consumption compared to those utilizing low-energy protocols like Zigbee or Matter.
[grrrr] Zigbee or 'Thread'. Matter is the language the devices speak. Matter devices can communicate over Wi-Fi or Thread.
[S Baldrick] These are rebranded Tuya devices, which require a constant connection to Tuya Cloud to work.
[Mild October] Jumped onto this boat as I'm already using Tapo cameras.
[gakko] I liked these but read that when they fail they flicker on and off at extreme rapid speed. And so damage your equipment.
Dunno if still true but a failure mode like that is enough to put me off for a long time.
[danielh] I got it for delivery but I used OnePass. Got 1 of them to be delivered to Melbourne.
[danielh] Just got message from Bunnings that they needed to cancel my order as it is out of stock.
[Aeronz] I've got a few of these, and been a year so far without much issues. But I'd be keen to be recommended of any simple smart plugs that does away with the whole on/off functionality (and the main point of failure most of us seemingly want to avoid) and simply just measure power, and that can also be integrated in home assistant. I've heard about the manual hacks people do with the relays in IKEA plugs, and other suggestions like those Shelly products, but I'm kinda hoping/preferring for more a plug based design to simply measure power for each of my devices and nothing else.
[crentist] Yea same. I've got a couple of smart plugs sitting in the box for a year, and just tried to buy a couple of these just to measure appliances
[danielh] I actually have one hooked up to my car battery trickle charger. I use it to only turn on for 2 hours a day in the garage.
The other one is hooked up to my home PC.
[whitelie] Mostly night lights for the kids and put them on the Christmas lights on timers when we put those up.
Have one on a heater in the kid's room, one on an electric blanket. One on a lamp in our room so we don't have to get out of bed to turn it on/off.
For the battery chargers for my cordless tools so I don't leave them running for days if I forget they were on charge
Also good for when you're away to randomly turn lamps on/off.
[ONEMariachi] Yeah nice. I kept seeing people only really mention coffee machines and lamps and I already have smart globes in all my lighting. So seeing these extra uses is great. Cheers
[Hornpub] Heater, it is perfect . Pre heat before you come back to the room and set time range to make sure it stays off in case you forget.
[Melbourne Mama] Ordered 2 for click and collect. I am sure it will be cancelled by bunnings.
